steps.title: Calculate standard deviation of data set A
steps.content:
Mean of A: $\frac{8 + 11 + 12 + 15 + 21 + 27}{6} = \frac{94}{6} = \frac{47}{3}$
For each value, compute the squared deviation from the mean:$$\begin{align}\left(8 - \frac{47}{3}\right)^2 &= \left(-\frac{23}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{529}{9}\left(11 - \frac{47}{3}\right)^2 &= \left(-\frac{14}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{196}{9}\left(12 - \frac{47}{3}\right)^2 &= \left(-\frac{11}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{121}{9}\left(15 - \frac{47}{3}\right)^2 &= \left(-\frac{2}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{4}{9}\left(21 - \frac{47}{3}\right)^2 &= \left(\frac{16}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{256}{9}\left(27 - \frac{47}{3}\right)^2 &= \left(\frac{34}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{1156}{9}\end{align}$$Sum of squared deviations: $\frac{529 + 196 + 121 + 4 + 256 + 1156}{9} = \frac{2262}{9}$
Variance: $\frac{2262}{9 \times 6} = \frac{377}{9}$
Standard deviation: $SD_A = \sqrt{\frac{377}{9}} \approx 6.47$

steps.title: Identify why h = 28 matters
steps.content:
Notice that data set B's first five values are 9, 12, 13, 16, 22.

Each equals the corresponding value from A plus 1:
$9 = 8 + 1$, $12 = 11 + 1$, $13 = 12 + 1$, $16 = 15 + 1$, $22 = 21 + 1$
If the sixth value were $h = 28 = 27 + 1$, then every value in B would equal the corresponding value in A plus 1.

When we add the same constant to all values in a data set, the mean shifts by that constant, but the deviations from the mean (and thus the standard deviation) remain unchanged.

Therefore, if $h = 28$, then $SD_B = SD_A$, which violates the condition $SD_A > SD_B$.

This means $h \neq 28$.

steps.title: Test the value $h = 23$
steps.content:
Data set B with $h = 23$: {9, 12, 13, 16, 22, 23}
Mean: $\bar{x}_B = \frac{9 + 12 + 13 + 16 + 22 + 23}{6} = \frac{95}{6}$
Sum of squared deviations:$$\frac{1681 + 529 + 289 + 1 + 1369 + 1849}{36} = \frac{5718}{36}$$Variance: $\frac{5718}{36 \times 6} = \frac{953}{36} \approx 26.47$
Standard deviation: $SD_B = \sqrt{\frac{953}{36}} \approx 5.15$
Since $5.15 < 6.47$, we have $SD_A > SD_B$. The value $h = 23$ satisfies the condition.

steps.title: Test h = 29 and identify answer
steps.content:
Data set B with $h = 29$: {9, 12, 13, 16, 22, 29}
Mean: $\bar{x}_B = \frac{9 + 12 + 13 + 16 + 22 + 29}{6} = \frac{101}{6}$
Sum of squared deviations:$$\frac{2209 + 841 + 529 + 25 + 961 + 5329}{36} = \frac{9894}{36}$$Variance: $\frac{9894}{36 \times 6} = \frac{1649}{36} \approx 45.81$
Standard deviation: $SD_B = \sqrt{\frac{1649}{36}} \approx 6.77$
Since $6.77 > 6.47$, we have $SD_A < SD_B$. The value $h = 29$ does not satisfy the condition.

Of the three values tested, only option I ($h = 23$) satisfies $SD_A > SD_B$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
